commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=be6817cc88a6e1441be66ce21b3dc72232ea0c36)
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes `chat.send`'s `sessionId` through reply options into `initSessionState` and adds gateway/session tests for matching and stale asserted session ids.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +91. Total +101 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge blocker: source inspection shows Control UI stores `currentSessionId`, sends it on normal `chat.send`, and the PR treats a matching requested id as fresh even when configured freshness is stale. I did not rerun a physical Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome wake cycle.
commands.review_followup: - A maintainer should decide the Control UI resume contract or ask the author to narrow the patch; automation cannot safely choose when a selected session may override configured resets.

target.title: fix #87700: [Bug]: Control UI webchat session silently resets after network disconnect / sleep — previous session file renamed to .jsonl.reset.* and lost from sessions tab
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI webchat could keep showing an existing conversation after reconnect, but the next send could be treated as an expired session and roll over the transcript.
- Solution: carry the existing `chat.send` `sessionId` into reply initialization and reuse the current store entry when the client asserts the same session id.
- What changed: `requestedSessionId` now flows from gateway chat send options into `initSessionState`, with gateway and session regression tests including the stale-id safety case.
- What did NOT change: explicit `/new` or idle expiry without a matching client session id still creates a new session.
- Fixes #87700

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** after a sleep/disconnect cycle, Control UI can still hold and send the currently displayed session id, but the gateway reply initialization previously ignored that id and could treat an idle-expired entry as stale, creating a new session and renaming the old transcript.
- **Real environment tested:** live local OpenClaw dev gateway started from this PR worktree on Linux, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`/state dir and a real WebSocket `connect` + `chat.send` request. The proof pre-seeded a stale `sessions.json` entry and transcript file, paired a temporary signed webchat device, sent through the running gateway, then inspected the actual session store and sessions directory.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_REPO="$TASK_WORKTREE" start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh
  node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/live-gateway-session-proof.mjs"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
  git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  gatewayConnect= ok
  chatSend= ok
  storeSessionBefore= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  storeSessionAfter= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  resetFiles= 0
  proof= live gateway chat.send preserved the asserted webchat session without .reset rollover

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  29 passed (29)

  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  98 passed (98)

  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 49.32s
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the running gateway accepted the real webchat `chat.send`, kept the `agent:main:main` store entry on the original session id, and produced zero `.reset.*` transcript files. The regression tests also prove `chat.send` forwards `sessionId` into reply initialization, matching requested ids preserve the current session, and stale/missing requested ids still follow the normal reset path.
- **What was not tested:** I did not reproduce a physical Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome wake cycle. The live proof covers the same gateway/WebSocket send path and session-store rollover decision in a local isolated gateway process.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** gateway `chat.send` preserves the caller-provided `sessionId`, the session initializer reuses an idle-expired entry only when that requested id matches the current entry, and a stale asserted id still resets.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the issue is caused by the handoff between the webchat gateway request and session reset policy, so the tests cover both the boundary wiring and the reset decision while the live proof covers the running gateway path.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `chat.send` already parsed the client-supplied `sessionId`, but reply initialization did not receive it. After a disconnect or sleep window, `initSessionState` only evaluated the stored entry against idle/daily freshness, so a still-displayed webchat session could be marked stale on the next message and the previous transcript could be rolled into a `.reset.*` file.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the gateway now carries the current client session assertion into the single session lifecycle decision point. `initSessionState` reuses only the existing entry whose stored `sessionId` exactly matches the requested id, so reconnecting webchat sends preserve continuity while stale or mismatched requests still follow the normal reset path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The `return undefined` lines are mock reply implementations in tests so `chat.send` can reach and expose the captured options without running a model call; the real gateway proof exercises the running WebSocket/RPC path and session store without relying on those mocks.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The session store remains the source of truth. This patch does not add a new session store or config surface; it passes an already-existing gateway request field into the existing session lifecycle owner.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
